summary: Event probabilities, damage ranges, agent counts, trigger conditions, and shelter mechanics all diverge from the spec.

evidence.detail: Tropical storm probability is 0.08 in code (event-system.ts:20) but spec requires 0.05 (5%).
Resource discovery probability is 0.12 in code (event-system.ts:43) but spec requires 0.08 (8%).
Illness outbreak probability is 0.05 in code (event-system.ts:65) but spec requires 0.04 (4%).
Hidden idol probability is 0.03 in code (event-system.ts:83) but spec requires 0.06 (6%).
Tropical storm damage range is 5-15 (event-system.ts:24) but spec requires 10-20 for unsheltered agents.
Tropical storm applies damage to ALL agents (event-system.ts:27-30) instead of only unsheltered agents; no shelter quality reduction or resource scattering is implemented.
Illness outbreak affects only 1 agent (event-system.ts:69) with 5-15 damage; spec requires 1-3 agents with 10-25 damage.
Spec requires trigger conditions (storms only when shelters exist, illness only when agents > 3) but code has no such guards.
No perception integration: spec requires affected agents receive event descriptions in their perception (except hidden idol), but no such mechanism exists in the code.
Hidden idol placement does not create an actual discoverable idol record; no integration with an 'explore' action exists.
Tests verify events fire and log but do not validate correct probabilities, damage ranges, agent counts, shelter mechanics, or trigger conditions.

issues: All four event probabilities differ from spec values (storm: 0.08 vs 0.05, resource: 0.12 vs 0.08, illness: 0.05 vs 0.04, idol: 0.03 vs 0.06)
Tropical storm does not check shelter status, does not reduce shelter quality, does not scatter resources, and uses wrong damage range (5-15 vs 10-20)
Illness outbreak affects 1 agent instead of 1-3, with wrong damage range (5-15 vs 10-25)
Missing trigger conditions: storms should require shelters to exist, illness should require agents > 3
No agent perception integration for event announcements
Hidden idol is not actually persisted as a discoverable game object and has no explore-action integration
Tests do not assert spec-mandated numeric values (probabilities, damage ranges, agent counts)
